rubric_items.reasoning: Root-cause engineering (Parallel podManagementPolicy + publishNotReadyAddresses correctly resolves the ES master-election/DNS chicken-and-egg deadlock), and the stage's own oracle confirmed green 3/3 cluster with working HTTPS read/write. Docked from 1.0 because the log shows es-0 freshly recreated (28s age) versus 11m for its peers, hinting at an unexplained mid-course restart, and the post-workflow regression sweep found 0 pods for this same case later on — most plausibly explained by later workflow stages (e.g. rollback/audit) rather than a defect in this submission, but not fully verifiable from the truncated log.

Agent diagnosed the two bootstrap deadlocks explicitly before acting, then verified methodically (poll for Ready → cluster health → write → read). Small detour: a write succeeded but an immediate read returned no hits until an explicit _refresh was issued, and the es-0 pod recreation suggests some in-flight troubleshooting not fully shown in the truncated log.

Commands were targeted and scoped (health checks, indexing, refresh, search, a bounded polling loop) with only 9 mutations for 5 created resources. Minor smell: hardcoded '-u elastic:elasticpass' during verification instead of reading the value from the existing elastic-password secret, though harmless in effect.
Created exactly the resources required (ConfigMap, headless Service, client Service, PDB, StatefulSet) with no extraneous mutations, keeping blast radius confined to the elasticsearch namespace.
